title: A Turning Point for Maternity Care in England
subtitle: The government promises urgent action after a major report highlights systemic failures in the NHS.

summary.en: A new report by Valerie Amos exposes serious safety issues within maternity services in England. The review identifies chronic understaffing and a harmful culture as primary reasons for poor care. Health Secretary James Murray has promised a complete reset of the system to prioritize patient safety. A new maternity commissioner will be appointed to lead these urgent improvements. This action follows several high-profile scandals involving preventable deaths and injuries to mothers and babies.
summary.zh: 由艾姆斯女男爵撰寫的一份新報告，揭露了英格蘭孕產婦服務中嚴重的安全問題。該審查將長期人力不足與有害的職場文化，認定為照護品質低落的主要原因。衛生大臣莫瑞已承諾對體系進行全面重整，以將病患安全置於首位。政府將任命一位新的孕產婦專員來領導這些緊急改進措施。此舉是在發生多起涉及孕產婦與嬰兒可預防死亡及傷害的高調醜聞後所採取的行動。

paragraphs.en: A major new report has called for an urgent change in how the National Health Service (NHS) provides care for mothers and babies in England. The review, led by Valerie Amos, describes a system that has failed to keep up with modern needs. Health Secretary James Murray has called the report a “watershed moment”—a point in time when things must change for the better—and promised that the government will take immediate action to fix these deep-rooted problems.
paragraphs.zh: 一份重要的最新報告呼籲，英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS）在提供孕產婦與嬰兒照護的方式上，必須進行緊急變革。這項由艾姆斯女男爵（Valerie Amos）領導的審查報告指出，現行體系已無法跟上現代需求。衛生大臣莫瑞（James Murray）將此報告稱為「分水嶺時刻」——即事情必須往更好方向發展的關鍵點——並承諾政府將立即採取行動，以解決這些根深蒂固的問題。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Health, the report highlights several alarming statistics. The number of mothers who die during or shortly after childbirth has risen by 20% compared to the period between 2009 and 2011. Furthermore, serious complications, such as severe bleeding and physical injuries during delivery, have become more common. Data shows that the UK now has one of the highest maternal death rates among eight European countries, with mothers in the UK being three times more likely to die during pregnancy than those in Norway.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報健康版》報導，該報告強調了幾項令人震驚的統計數據。與二〇〇九年至二〇一一年期間相比，在分娩期間或分娩後不久死亡的母親人數增加了百分之二十。此外，嚴重的併發症，例如生產過程中的大出血與身體損傷，也變得更加普遍。數據顯示，英國目前的孕產婦死亡率在八個歐洲國家中名列前茅，英國母親在懷孕期間死亡的機率是挪威母親的三倍。

paragraphs.en: Why is this happening? The report points to several factors, including chronic understaffing. The Royal College of Midwives reports that the NHS is short of 2,500 midwives. Additionally, inspections by the Care Quality Commission found that nearly half of all maternity services in England require improvement or are simply inadequate. Beyond staffing, the report identifies a toxic culture within many hospitals. According to The Guardian Health, hospital leaders often prioritize their reputation over the safety of patients. This leads to a situation where women are ignored, and mistakes are hidden rather than addressed openly.
paragraphs.zh: 為什麼會發生這種情況？報告指出多項因素，包括長期的醫療人力不足。英國皇家助產士學院指出，國家醫療保健服務目前短缺兩千五百名助產士。此外，照護品質委員會的視察發現，英格蘭近半數的婦產科服務需要改進，或是根本不合格。除了人力問題，報告還點出許多醫院內部存在有害的職場文化。據《衛報健康版》報導，醫院領導層往往將自身聲譽置於病患安全之上。這導致女性被忽視，而錯誤被掩蓋而非公開處理的情況發生。

paragraphs.en: This investigation follows a series of tragic scandals that have shaken public trust. Previous reviews, such as those by Donna Ockenden, revealed that hundreds of babies died or suffered brain damage due to poor care at trusts in Shrewsbury, Telford, and Nottingham. These cases involved preventable errors that caused immense pain to families. The current review by Lady Amos aims to stop this cycle by creating one set of national standards to ensure that every mother receives the same high level of care, no matter where they live.
paragraphs.zh: 這項調查是在一系列動搖公眾信任的悲劇醜聞之後進行的。先前的審查，例如由歐肯登（Donna Ockenden）所進行的調查，揭露了在什魯斯伯里、特爾福德與諾丁漢的醫療信託機構中，數百名嬰兒因照護不當而死亡或遭受腦部損傷。這些案例涉及可預防的錯誤，對家庭造成了巨大的痛苦。艾姆斯女男爵目前的審查旨在透過建立一套全國性標準來終結這種惡性循環，確保每位母親無論居住在何處，都能獲得同樣高水準的照護。

paragraphs.en: To address these issues, the government has agreed to recruit the UK’s first-ever maternity commissioner. This person will work with the health secretary to lead a national taskforce and ensure that the voices of families are heard at the highest levels of government. James Murray stated that the role of the commissioner will be to push for an urgent transformation of childbirth services. While the specific person for this role has not been confirmed, many expect it to be Donna Ockenden, given her extensive work on previous maternity inquiries.
paragraphs.zh: 為了應對這些問題，政府已同意聘請英國史上首位孕產婦專員。此人將與衛生大臣合作，領導一個國家級專案小組，並確保家庭的聲音能在政府最高層級被聽見。莫瑞表示，專員的角色將是推動分娩服務的緊急轉型。雖然該職位的人選尚未確認，但鑑於歐肯登在先前孕產婦調查中的廣泛工作經驗，許多人預期將由她擔任。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the appointment of a commissioner, the government plans to dismantle the "toxic dynamics" that currently exist between hospital staff. The report suggests that the system has struggled to adapt to major changes, such as the fact that women are becoming mothers at an older age and that the number of caesarean sections—a surgical procedure to deliver a baby—has risen significantly. In fact, for the first time, more babies in England were born via caesarean section than through vaginal birth last year.
paragraphs.zh: 除了任命專員外，政府計畫瓦解目前醫院員工之間存在的「有害互動模式」。報告指出，該體系難以適應重大變化，例如女性生育年齡提高，以及剖腹產——一種透過手術進行分娩的程序——的數量顯著增加。事實上，去年英格蘭透過剖腹產出生的嬰兒數量首次超過了自然產。

paragraphs.en: Moving forward, the government is under pressure to ensure that these recommendations do not simply sit on a shelf. The health secretary emphasized that the culture of the NHS must change from the top down. Hospital executives and senior doctors are being told to put aside professional rivalries and focus entirely on the safety of women and children. A detailed action plan is expected to be published in December, which will outline the next steps for this national overhaul.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，政府面臨壓力，必須確保這些建議不會只是束之高閣。衛生大臣強調，國家醫療保健服務的文化必須由上而下進行改變。醫院高層與資深醫師被要求放下專業競爭，將全部心力集中在婦女與兒童的安全上。一份詳細的行動計畫預計將於十二月發布，其中將概述這項全國性改革的後續步驟。

paragraphs.en: For many families who have experienced the failures of the current system, these promises are long overdue. The goal is to restore faith in a service that is meant to support one of the most important moments in a person’s life. As the government begins this process, the focus will remain on whether these structural changes can effectively reduce the number of preventable deaths and injuries across the country. The success of this initiative will be measured by its ability to turn these strong words into safer, more reliable care for every mother and baby in the NHS.
paragraphs.zh: 對於許多經歷過現行體系失靈的家庭來說，這些承諾早已遲到。目標是恢復人們對一項旨在支持人生最重要時刻之一的服務的信心。隨著政府啟動此程序，焦點將持續放在這些結構性變革是否能有效減少全國範圍內可預防的死亡與傷害。這項計畫的成功與否，將取決於其能否將這些強而有力的言詞，轉化為國家醫療保健服務中每位母親與嬰兒更安全、更可靠的照護。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be unable to change or improve at the same speed as something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語在文中表達系統無法適應現代需求。文中：The review, led by Valerie Amos, describes a system that has failed to keep up with modern needs.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be ignored or not put into action after being created or suggested.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 被擱置、束之高閣（指計畫或建議未被執行）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是放在架子上，這裡比喻建議被忽略而不被執行。文中：Moving forward, the government is under pressure to ensure that these recommendations do not simply sit on a shelf.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Involving the most senior people in an organization to influence those below them.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 由上而下（指組織改革或決策）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 形容改革必須從高層開始推動。文中：The health secretary emphasized that the culture of the NHS must change from the top down.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: What does the Amos report reveal about maternity and neonatal care in England?

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Damning report on England maternity care ‘watershed moment’, health secretary says

previousArticles.title: Government Promises Major Changes After Damning Report on Maternity Care
previousArticles.summaryEn: A new government-ordered report has revealed serious failings in maternity care across England. The review found that many women are not being listened to, and that racism and discrimination are present in the system. Health Secretary James Murray has promised a radical overhaul, including new emergency standards and more staff. However, some families and experts have criticized the proposed solution of a new maternity commissioner. The report follows a series of high-profile scandals involving preventable deaths and injuries to mothers and babies.

previousArticles.title: Resident Doctors in England End Long-Running Strikes
previousArticles.summaryEn: Resident doctors in England have voted to accept a government offer on pay and working conditions. This decision officially ends three years of strikes that caused significant disruption to patient care. The new package includes pay increases, better career progression, and 4,500 additional training places. Both government officials and union leaders expressed relief that the long-running dispute has finally reached a resolution. The focus now shifts to implementing these changes to help rebuild the health service.

quiz.question: According to the report, what is one of the primary reasons for the decline in the quality of maternity care in England?

options.A: A significant decrease in the number of women choosing to have children.
options.B: A chronic shortage of approximately 2,500 midwives within the NHS.
options.C: The government's decision to stop funding maternity services in rural areas.
options.D: The refusal of families to report medical errors to hospital authorities.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出「The Royal College of Midwives reports that the NHS is short of 2,500 midwives」，這是導致照護品質下降的具體原因之一。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到的是產婦年齡變大，而非人數減少。選項 C 在文中未提及，屬於無中生有。選項 D 錯誤，文中提到的是醫院領導層隱瞞錯誤，而非家庭拒絕回報。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current relationship between hospital leadership and patient safety?

options.A: Hospital leaders are fully aware of the issues but lack the budget to fix them.
options.B: The priority of hospital management has often shifted away from patient welfare to protect institutional image.
options.C: Hospital executives are actively working with the government to recruit a new maternity commissioner.
options.D: The toxic culture is primarily caused by the rivalry between midwives and senior doctors.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第三段，文章提到醫院領導層常將「聲譽（reputation）」置於「病患安全（safety）」之上，導致錯誤被隱瞞。這可以推論出醫院管理層為了維護形象而犧牲了安全。選項 A 無法由文中推論。選項 C 雖提到政府要招募專員，但未提及醫院主管與此事的合作過程。選項 D 將「toxic culture」簡化為特定職位間的競爭，過於武斷且不全面。

quiz.question: In the first paragraph, what does the phrase 'watershed moment' mean in the context of the NHS report?

options.A: A period of heavy financial loss for the government.
options.B: A time when the system is operating at its maximum capacity.
options.C: A critical turning point that necessitates significant reform.
options.D: A moment of deep sadness regarding past medical failures.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文中括號解釋了該詞為「a point in time when things must change for the better」，意指一個關鍵的轉捩點，必須進行變革。選項 A 與財務損失無直接關係。選項 B 的「最大產能」並未捕捉到「必須改變」的語意。選項 D 雖然提到悲劇，但 watershed moment 強調的是未來改革的必要性，而非單純的情感表達。

quiz.question: What is the primary goal of the new report and the government's proposed actions?

options.A: To investigate the personal history of hospital executives in Shrewsbury and Nottingham.
options.B: To replace all current midwives with new staff to improve hospital culture.
options.C: To provide financial compensation to all families affected by past scandals.
options.D: To implement national standards and structural changes to ensure safer maternity care.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。綜合全文，報告與政府行動的核心目的在於解決系統性問題，透過建立全國統一標準（national standards）與指派專員，旨在改善照護品質並減少可預防的傷害。選項 A 只是過去調查的一部分，非整體目標。選項 B 提到更換所有助產士，文中並未如此建議。選項 C 雖提及家庭痛苦，但政府的目標是結構性改革，而非單純的賠償。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
